results.title: Cooperation Activities
results._source_text_exact: 1.1 The Parties shall collaborate on Cooperation Activities as mutually agreed from time to time in a written project plan, which shall specify the scope, timeline, budget, and responsibilities of each Party.
results.original_text: 1.1 The Parties shall collaborate on Cooperation Activities as mutually agreed from time to time in a written project plan, which shall specify the scope, timeline, budget, and responsibilities of each Party.
results.reasoning_evidence: 1.1 The Parties shall collaborate on Cooperation Activities as mutually agreed from time to time in a written project plan, which shall specify the scope, timel
results.quoted_text: The Parties shall collaborate on Cooperation Activities as mutually agreed from time to time in a written project plan, which shall specify the scope, timeline, budget, and responsibilities of each Party.
results.explanation_simple: The clause outlines a collaborative framework for the Parties to engage in activities as defined in a project plan.
results.why_it_matters: It establishes a structured approach to collaboration, ensuring clarity on responsibilities and expectations.
results.legal_insight: The clause promotes cooperation by requiring a written project plan, which can help prevent misunderstandings about each Party's obligations.
results.recommendation: The clause's requirement for a written project plan is reasonable as it clarifies the scope and responsibilities.
section_path: 1. SCOPE OF COOPERATION

1.1 The Parties shall collaborate on Cooperation Activities as mutually agreed from time to time in a written project plan, which shall specify the scope, timeline, budget, and responsibilities of each Party.

legal_insight.text_before_validation: The clause promotes cooperation by requiring a written project plan, which can help prevent misunderstandings about each Party's obligations.

recommendation.text_before_validation: The clause's requirement for a written project plan is reasonable as it clarifies the scope and responsibilities.

results.title: Cost Allocation
results._source_text_exact: 2.1 Unless otherwise specified in a project plan, each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with the Cooperation Activities. Any shared costs shall be split equally unless otherwise agreed in writing.
results.original_text: 2.1 Unless otherwise specified in a project plan, each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with the Cooperation Activities. Any shared costs shall be split equally unless otherwise agreed in writing.
results.reasoning_evidence: 2.1 Unless otherwise specified in a project plan, each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with the Cooperation Activities. Any s
results.quoted_text: Each Party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with the Cooperation Activities. Any shared costs shall be split equally unless otherwise agreed in writing.
results.explanation_simple: Each party is responsible for its own costs, and shared costs are to be split equally.
results.why_it_matters: This clause clarifies financial responsibilities, reducing potential disputes over cost allocation.
results.legal_insight: Manual review required: generated text did not pass source-fidelity validation.
results.market_comparison: This cost allocation approach is common in collaborative agreements.

results.title: Intellectual Property Ownership
results._source_text_exact: 3.1 Each Party retains ownership of its pre-existing intellectual property. Joint IP shall be jointly owned by the Parties in equal, undivided shares, with each Party entitled to exploit the Joint IP without accounting to the other, subject to a right of first negotiation before licensing Joint IP to a direct competitor of the other Party.
results.original_text: 3.1 Each Party retains ownership of its pre-existing intellectual property. Joint IP shall be jointly owned by the Parties in equal, undivided shares, with each Party entitled to exploit the Joint IP without accounting to the other, subject to a right of first negotiation before licensing Joint IP to a direct competitor of the other Party.
results.reasoning_evidence: 3.1 Each Party retains ownership of its pre-existing intellectual property. Joint IP shall be jointly owned by the Parties in equal, undivided shares, with each
results.quoted_text: Each Party retains ownership of its pre-existing intellectual property. Joint IP shall be jointly owned by the Parties in equal, undivided shares, with each Party entitled to exploit the Joint IP without accounting to the other, subject to a right of first negotiation before licensing Joint IP to a direct competitor of the other Party.
results.explanation_simple: The clause establishes ownership rights for pre-existing and joint intellectual property.
results.why_it_matters: It clarifies how intellectual property created before and during the agreement is owned and exploited, which is crucial for both Parties' business interests.
results.legal_insight: This clause allocates ownership, use rights, licensing rights, or control over created or pre-existing intellectual property.
results.recommendation: Confirm ownership of pre-existing materials, newly created deliverables, license scope, usage restrictions, moral rights, and post-termination rights.

results.title: Confidentiality Obligations

results.quoted_text: Each Party shall keep confidential any non-public information disclosed by the other Party in connection with this Agreement and shall use such information solely for purposes of the Cooperation Activities.
results.explanation_simple: The clause mandates confidentiality regarding non-public information shared between the parties.
results.why_it_matters: Maintaining confidentiality protects sensitive information and fosters trust between the parties.
results.legal_insight: This clause restricts what the receiving party may actually do with the confidential information, independent of whether it keeps it secret.
results.market_comparison: The clause aligns with common confidentiality practices in commercial agreements.

results.title: Term and Termination
results._source_text_exact: 6.1 This Agreement shall continue for two (2) years from the Effective Date, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provides sixty (60) days' written notice of non-renewal. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon ninety (90) days' written notice.
results.original_text: 6.1 This Agreement shall continue for two (2) years from the Effective Date, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provides sixty (60) days' written notice of non-renewal. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon ninety (90) days' written notice.
results.reasoning_evidence: 6.1 This Agreement shall continue for two (2) years from the Effective Date, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provid
results.quoted_text: This Agreement shall continue for two (2) years from the Effective Date, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provides sixty (60) days' written notice of non-renewal. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon ninety (90) days' written notice.
results.explanation_simple: The clause includes automatic renewal and termination rights.
results.why_it_matters: Automatic renewal can lead to unintended extensions of the Agreement, while the termination notice period affects flexibility.
results.legal_insight: This clause controls how the contract continues, renews, extends, or ends after the initial term.
results.safer_alternative: The Agreement shall continue for two (2) years from the Effective Date, with renewal subject to mutual agreement at least sixty (60) days prior to the end of the term.

section_path: 6. TERM AND TERMINATION

6.1 This Agreement shall continue for two (2) years from the Effective Date, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provides sixty (60) days' written notice of non-renewal. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon ninety (90) days' written notice.
risk_reasons: Termination provisions may affect operational continuity or legal remedies.
Renewal provisions may affect contract duration, exit rights, or future pricing leverage.

clauses.title: Term and Termination
clauses._source_text_exact: 6.1 This Agreement shall continue for two (2) years from the Effective Date, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provides sixty (60) days' written notice of non-renewal. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon ninety (90) days' written notice.

section_path: 6. TERM AND TERMINATION

6.1 This Agreement shall continue for two (2) years from the Effective Date, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provides sixty (60) days' written notice of non-renewal. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon ninety (90) days' written notice.

Renewal provisions may affect contract duration, exit rights, or future pricing leverage.

clauses.title: Cooperation Activities

clauses.legal_insight: The clause promotes cooperation by requiring a written project plan, which can help prevent misunderstandings about each Party's obligations.
clauses.recommendation: The clause's requirement for a written project plan is reasonable as it clarifies the scope and responsibilities.

clauses.title: Intellectual Property Ownership
clauses._source_text_exact: 3.1 Each Party retains ownership of its pre-existing intellectual property. Joint IP shall be jointly owned by the Parties in equal, undivided shares, with each Party entitled to exploit the Joint IP without accounting to the other, subject to a right of first negotiation before licensing Joint IP to a direct competitor of the other Party.

section_path: 6. TERM AND TERMINATION

6.1 This Agreement shall continue for two (2) years from the Effective Date, renewing automatically for successive one (1) year terms unless either Party provides sixty (60) days' written notice of non-renewal. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon ninety (90) days' written notice.
risk_reasons: Termination provisions may affect operational continuity or legal remedies.
Renewal provisions may affect contract duration, exit rights, or future pricing leverage.

key_dependencies.reason: Confidentiality breaches may be excluded from liability caps.
